title: Working with Modeled Properties
text: Working with Modeled Properties Create modeled properties using editors available on the More Editors menu. Use a modeled property by clicking the More icon ( ) in the appropriate property of an entity or field. To create a modeled property: In the Uniface IDE, click the More Editors and choose the Modeled property. One of: Entity Interface Field Interface Field Syntax Field Layout Enter a Name and optional Description . Set the other properties, as appropriate. For information about the properties, press F1 to display the online help. To save the modeled property, choose File >Save . Alternatively, click OK to save it and exit the editor. Modeled properties are implicitly defined in an untyped library called LIB . To use a modeled property, when editing an entity or field: In the Properties Inspector, click the More icon ( ) of the relevant property: Database Interface Field Syntax Field Layout Click Use modeled to browse and select the modeled property you want to use. The property now references a modeled property. If the modeled property changes and the entity or component is recompiled, it will inherit the modified modeled property. Related Links Modeled Entity Interface A Modeled Entity Interface is a Development Object that defines a reusable value for the Database Interface property of a Database Entity. Modeled Field Interface A Modeled Field Interface is a Development Object that defines a reusable value for the Database Interface property of a Database Field. Modeled Field Layout A Modeled Field Layout is a Development Object that defines the way data is displayed. Modeled Field Syntax A Modeled Field Syntax is a Development Object that defines some validation rules for data entry in the Field, such as the allowed characters, and field display and control attributes, such dimming and auto-jump.
